questions.question: Why do you want to study in Canada?
questions.suggestedAnswer: I want to study in Canada because {{why_this_field}}. The Canadian education system offers superior {{field_of_study}} programs and opportunities that will help me achieve my career goals in a way that is not available in {{country_of_residence}}.
questions.guidance: Explain what Canada offers that your home country cannot. Focus on educational advantages, not immigration intent.
questions.tooltip: Officers assess genuine educational motivation vs. immigration intent.
variations: What are your reasons for pursuing your education in Canada?
Could you elaborate on your decision to select Canada for your studies?
What specifically attracts you to Canada as a country for higher education?
Why have you chosen Canada over other countries for your academic pursuits?

questions.question: What will be your major or field of study?
questions.suggestedAnswer: I will major in {{field_of_study}}.
questions.guidance: Be clear and specific about your major and intended specialization.
questions.tooltip: Demonstrates a well-thought-out educational plan.
variations: Which academic discipline do you intend to pursue?
Could you specify your intended area of specialization?
What is the particular field of study you plan to enroll in?
In which major will you be undertaking your program?

questions.question: Why did you choose this field of study?
questions.suggestedAnswer: I chose {{field_of_study}} because {{why_this_field}}. This field aligns with my interests and long-term career aspirations.
questions.guidance: Show genuine passion and connection to your field. Explain what drew you to it.
questions.tooltip: Demonstrates thoughtful motivation for field selection.
variations: What motivated you to select this specific academic discipline?
Could you explain your reasoning behind choosing this particular field?
What led you to decide on this area for your advanced studies?
Why is this field of study of particular interest to you?

questions.question: Tell us about your previous education.
questions.suggestedAnswer: I completed my {{current_education_level}} with a GPA of {{previous_gpa}}. {{previous_education_details}} This educational foundation has prepared me well for advanced studies.
questions.guidance: Describe your educational timeline, GPA, and notable achievements.
questions.tooltip: Officers verify academic preparation for higher education.
variations: Could you provide a summary of your academic background?
Please describe your educational history prior to this application.
What are your qualifications from your earlier schooling?
Can you outline your past academic achievements and institutions?

questions.question: Why are you planning to continue your education in Canada? Can you not continue in your home country?
questions.suggestedAnswer: While I could continue my education in {{country_of_residence}}, Canada offers superior {{field_of_study}} programs, world-renowned professors, and research opportunities that are unmatched. This will significantly enhance my qualifications.
questions.guidance: Be honest about limitations in your home country while explaining Canada-specific advantages.
questions.tooltip: Officers want to understand why Canadian education is specifically necessary.
variations: What are your reasons for seeking further education in Canada, rather than pursuing it domestically?
Why have you opted to advance your studies in Canada, and what limitations prevent you from doing so in your home country?
Could you explain your choice to pursue your continued education abroad in Canada, and why similar opportunities are unavailable or less suitable at home?
What specific advantages does continuing your education in Canada offer you, compared to the options available in your country of origin?

questions.question: How will this study program relate to your past work or studies?
questions.suggestedAnswer: My {{intended_degree}} at {{primary_university}} will advance my knowledge in {{field_of_study}} and enable me to {{post_graduation_job_plans}}. This represents a clear progression from my previous experience.
questions.guidance: Draw a clear connection between your past and future, showing coherent progression.
questions.tooltip: Demonstrates a coherent educational and career plan.
variations: In what ways does this academic program align with your prior educational or professional background?
Could you articulate the connection between your previous academic and work experiences and this chosen course of study?
How does this program serve as a natural progression from your former studies or work history?
What is the relevance of this program to your existing academic qualifications or professional experience?

questions.question: How many universities did you apply to?
questions.suggestedAnswer: I applied to {{total_universities_applied}} universities in total.
questions.guidance: State the exact number of universities you applied to.
questions.tooltip: Shows application selectivity and planning.
variations: To how many educational institutions did you submit applications?
Could you state the total number of universities where you sought admission?
Aside from this institution, how many other universities did you apply to?
What was the extent of your university application process in terms of numbers?

questions.question: Why did you choose this university as your primary choice?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{why_primary_university}} This university stands out because of its program strength, faculty expertise, and alignment with my academic and career goals.
questions.guidance: Explain what makes this university unique for you and your field.
questions.tooltip: Demonstrates genuine interest and informed decision-making.
variations: What led you to designate this university as your top preference?
Could you explain your specific reasons for selecting this institution as your first choice?
Why is this particular university your preferred institution for study?
What factors influenced your decision to prioritize this university above others?

questions.question: What do you know about your university's program?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{university_knowledge}} I have researched the program curriculum, campus facilities, and community resources, and I am confident that this university is the right fit for me.
questions.guidance: Demonstrate detailed research about curriculum, facilities, professors, and campus life.
questions.tooltip: Shows genuine interest and preparation for arrival.
variations: Could you describe your understanding of the curriculum for your chosen program at this university?
What specific details have you learned about the program you intend to pursue here?
Please share your knowledge regarding the academic structure and content of your program.
What information have you gathered about the specifics of your university program?

questions.question: What do you know about professors or faculty in your field?
questions.suggestedAnswer: I have researched faculty in the {{field_of_study}} department at {{primary_university}}. I am particularly interested in learning from professors who specialize in that field.
questions.guidance: Mention specific professors or show knowledge of faculty research areas.
questions.tooltip: Demonstrates genuine academic interest in the institution.
variations: Could you tell us about any faculty members or professors in your area of study whom you are familiar with?
What information have you gathered about the academic staff specializing in your chosen field at this institution?
Are you aware of any specific professors or faculty members relevant to your discipline here?
Please share what you know regarding the expertise or research of the faculty in your department.

questions.question: In which city is your university located?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{primary_university}} is located in {{university_city_state}}.
questions.guidance: Mention city clearly. In the Canada, this is typically in Ontario, Quebec, British Columbia, or other provinces.
questions.tooltip: Verifies university location knowledge and familiarity with Canadian geography.
variations: In which city is your prospective university situated?
Could you please specify the urban center where your chosen university is located?
Where is the campus of your academic institution geographically situated?
What is the city of location for the university you plan to attend?

questions.question: What are your English proficiency test scores (TOEFL or IELTS)?
questions.suggestedAnswer: I achieved a score of {{ielts_score ? '{{ielts_score}}' : '{{toefl_score}}'}} on the {{english_proficiency_test}} exam, which demonstrates my readiness for university-level coursework in English.
questions.guidance: State your test scores confidently and explain what they mean for your academic readiness.
questions.tooltip: Test scores are mandatory verification of English proficiency.
variations: Could you provide your scores from your English language proficiency examination (TOEFL or IELTS)?
What were the results of your standardized English test, such as TOEFL or IELTS?
Please share your official scores for the TOEFL or IELTS assessment.
What scores did you achieve on your English proficiency test (TOEFL or IELTS)?

questions.question: What was your GPA in your previous education?
questions.suggestedAnswer: My previous GPA was {{previous_gpa}}, which demonstrates my consistent academic performance and preparation for graduate-level work.
questions.guidance: State your GPA on the same scale as your previous institution.
questions.tooltip: Shows academic preparation for Canadian university standards.
variations: Could you tell me your cumulative GPA from your prior academic studies?
What was your grade point average during your most recent educational program?
Please state your academic GPA from your previous schooling.
What was the GPA you earned in your last educational institution?

questions.question: Was your previous education conducted in English?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{medium_of_instruction ? 'Yes, my previous education was conducted in English, which provides me a strong foundation for Canadian university studies.' : 'No, my education was not in English medium, however I have studied English extensively and achieved strong proficiency scores ({{english_proficiency_test}}: {{ielts_score ? ielts_score : toefl_score}}). I am well-prepared to succeed in English-medium academic coursework.'}}
questions.guidance: Answer honestly about your educational medium.
questions.tooltip: Determines English proficiency level and need for support.
variations: Was English the primary language of instruction for your prior academic program?
Did you receive your previous education through the medium of English?
Was the curriculum of your past studies delivered in English?
Was English used as the main teaching language in your previous educational experience?

questions.question: How confident are you in your English ability?
questions.suggestedAnswer: I would rate my English proficiency at {{english_confidence_rating}} out of 10. I have the foundation needed to succeed in English-medium coursework.
questions.guidance: Be honest about your comfort level with academic English.
questions.tooltip: Self-assessment of English readiness for Canadian university.
variations: How would you assess your confidence level in your English language skills?
On a scale of your own perception, how comfortable are you with your English proficiency?
What is your level of assurance regarding your English language capabilities?
How confident do you feel about your command of the English language?

questions.question: How will you manage studying in English at a Canadian university?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{how_you_will_manage_english}} I am committed to utilizing all available resources to ensure my academic success.
questions.guidance: Explain your strategies such as tutoring, study groups, or academic support services.
questions.tooltip: Demonstrates readiness to handle academic English challenges.
variations: What is your strategy for adapting to an all-English academic environment in Canada?
How do you plan to successfully navigate your studies conducted entirely in English at a Canadian institution?
Could you explain your approach to studying effectively in English at a Canadian university?
What steps will you take to ensure you can manage academic work in English in Canada?

questions.question: Why pursue your degree in Canada instead of other countries?
questions.suggestedAnswer: Canada offers a unique educational experience in {{field_of_study}} and {{primary_university}} specifically provides {{why_primary_university}}. While other countries have excellent programs, Canada aligns best with my career goals.
questions.tooltip: Shows thoughtful analysis of educational options.
variations: What are your specific reasons for choosing Canada as your study destination over other nations?
Could you elaborate on why you prefer to obtain your degree in Canada rather than elsewhere?
What motivates your decision to pursue your education in Canada, as opposed to other countries?
Why have you opted for Canada to undertake your studies, rather than another international destination?

questions.question: What will you do if you struggle academically in the first semester?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{academic_success_strategy}} I will also seek support from {{primary_university}} academic resources, meet with professors during office hours, form study groups, and utilize tutoring services if needed.
questions.guidance: Show proactive planning and knowledge of university support systems.
questions.tooltip: Demonstrates realistic expectations and commitment to success.
variations: What is your plan of action if you encounter academic difficulties during your initial semester?
How would you address any academic challenges you might face in your first term?
What measures will you take if you find yourself struggling with your studies in the opening semester?
Should you experience academic struggles in your first semester, what support mechanisms would you utilize?

questions.question: Who is your financial sponsor?
questions.suggestedAnswer: My {{sponsor_relationship}} will sponsor my education.
questions.guidance: Identify your main source of financial support clearly.
questions.tooltip: Critical to establish who is funding your education.
variations: Could you please identify the individual or entity responsible for your financial support?
Who will be funding your education and living expenses?
Who is providing the financial backing for your studies?
Please state the name of your financial guarantor.

questions.question: What is your sponsor's occupation?
questions.suggestedAnswer: My sponsor works as a {{sponsor_occupation}}.
questions.guidance: Describe the primary source of their income clearly.
questions.tooltip: Assesses financial stability and income source.
variations: What is the profession or job title of your financial sponsor?
Could you describe your sponsor's line of work?
What type of employment does your financial guarantor hold?
What is your sponsor's current job or occupation?

questions.question: What is your sponsor's annual income in USD?
questions.suggestedAnswer: My sponsor's annual income is approximately ${{sponsor_annual_income}}, which is sufficient to cover my education costs.
questions.guidance: Provide in USD, converting from local currency using current exchange rates.
questions.tooltip: Income is critical to prove financial capability for the full program duration.
variations: Could you please state your sponsor's total annual earnings in US dollars?
What is the yearly income of your financial sponsor, expressed in USD?
In USD, what is the reported annual revenue of your sponsor?
Can you provide the figure for your sponsor's yearly income in US currency?

questions.question: Do you have proof of your sponsor's income?
questions.suggestedAnswer: Yes, I have prepared comprehensive income documentation including {{sponsor_income_proof}} to verify my sponsor's financial capability for funding my education in Canada.
questions.guidance: Prepare tax returns (last 2 years), salary certificates, or business documents.
questions.tooltip: Essential documents for visa officer verification.
variations: Do you possess documentation verifying your sponsor's income?
Can you present evidence of your financial sponsor's earnings?
Is proof of your sponsor's annual income available?
Do you have documents to substantiate your sponsor's financial capacity?

questions.question: What is the total estimated cost of your program?
questions.suggestedAnswer: The total estimated cost of my {{intended_degree}} program is approximately ${{total_program_cost}} per year, including tuition, fees, accommodation, and living expenses.
questions.guidance: Break down costs into components to show you've researched thoroughly.
questions.tooltip: Cost breakdown shows realistic planning.
variations: What is the comprehensive estimated expenditure for your academic program?
Could you specify the overall anticipated cost of your entire course of study?
What are the projected total expenses for the duration of your program?
Please state the estimated full cost associated with your educational program.

questions.question: How much funding can you access for your education?
questions.suggestedAnswer: I have approximately ${{available_funds}} available for my education from my sponsor and personal resources.
questions.guidance: Include sponsor savings, family gifts, and any personal funds.
questions.tooltip: Must cover program cost to demonstrate financial capability.
variations: How much financial support do you have readily accessible for your studies?
What is the total amount of funding you can utilize for your education?
Could you indicate the sum of resources you can access for your academic pursuits?
What volume of funds is at your disposal to cover your educational expenses?

questions.question: How do you plan to fund your entire education?
questions.suggestedAnswer: My {{sponsor_relationship}} will provide approximately ${{available_funds}}, which covers the full cost of my education. {{scholarship_status ? 'I have also received a {{scholarship_type}} scholarship of ${{scholarship_amount}} from {{primary_university}}, which further strengthens my financial capability.' : 'I am financially supported by my sponsor and do not require additional scholarship funding.'}}
questions.guidance: Show complete picture: sponsor funds, scholarship (if any), and any personal contribution.
questions.tooltip: Officers need to see complete financing for all program years.
variations: What is your strategy for financing your complete education?
How do you intend to cover the full costs of your academic program?
Can you explain your plan for funding your entire course of study?
What sources and methods will you use to finance your education comprehensively?

questions.question: Do you have proof of available funds?
questions.suggestedAnswer: Yes, I have prepared comprehensive proof of available funds including {{funds_availability_proof}} that demonstrate my sponsor's and my financial capability to fund the complete cost of my studies in Canada.
questions.guidance: Prepare bank statements, affidavits of support, and investment documents.
questions.tooltip: Mandatory for CAS (Confirmation of Acceptance for Studies) and visa officer verification.
variations: Do you have documentary evidence of your available funds?
Can you provide verification of the funds you have access to?
Is there proof demonstrating the availability of your financial resources?
Do you possess documents to confirm your accessible funding?

questions.question: Did you receive a scholarship?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{scholarship_status ? 'Yes, I received a {{scholarship_type}} scholarship from {{primary_university}}, which significantly supports my education financially.' : 'No, I did not receive a scholarship, but my education is fully funded through my sponsor and personal resources.'}}
questions.guidance: Answer honestly about scholarship status.
questions.tooltip: Scholarships strengthen financial capability profile.
variations: Have you been awarded any scholarships?
Did you receive any financial aid in the form of a scholarship?
Was a scholarship granted to you?
Are you a recipient of a scholarship?

questions.question: What is your scholarship amount?
questions.suggestedAnswer: I received a ${{scholarship_amount}} {{scholarship_type}} scholarship from {{primary_university}}.
questions.guidance: State the annual or total amount as specified in your scholarship letter.
questions.tooltip: Shows partial or full financial support from university.
variations: What is the monetary value of your scholarship?
Could you state the total sum of your scholarship award?
What is the financial worth of the scholarship you received?
How much funding does your scholarship provide?

questions.question: What type of scholarship did you receive?
questions.suggestedAnswer: My scholarship is {{scholarship_type}}, which significantly reduces my financial burden for my education.
questions.guidance: Specify if it's merit-based, need-based, full tuition, etc.
questions.tooltip: Shows academic excellence or financial strength.
variations: Could you describe the nature of the scholarship you were awarded?
What category does your scholarship fall under?
What kind of scholarship did you receive?
Can you specify the type of scholarship granted to you?

questions.question: What if your sponsor cannot support you fully?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{funding_shortfall_plan}} I have researched alternative funding and identified multiple backup plans to ensure I can complete my education.
questions.guidance: Describe alternative sources: loans, additional family funds, part-time work, etc.
questions.tooltip: Backup plans show maturity and serious commitment.
variations: What alternative financial arrangements do you have if your sponsor's support is insufficient?
Should your sponsor be unable to provide full support, what is your contingency plan?
In the event your sponsor cannot fully fund your education, what is your backup plan?
If there's a shortfall in your sponsor's support, what is your plan to cover it?

questions.question: How does this course fit your long-term career goals?
questions.suggestedAnswer: My long-term career goal is to {{post_graduation_job_plans}}. This {{intended_degree}} in {{field_of_study}} is the ideal stepping stone to achieve this. The program will equip me with the {{why_this_field}} skills that are directly needed for this role.
questions.guidance: Draw a clear connection between the specific program and your career objectives.
questions.tooltip: Demonstrates that your study plans are purposeful and aligned with realistic career goals.
variations: How does this academic program align with your long-term professional aspirations?
In what way will pursuing this course contribute to achieving your future career objectives?
Can you explain the relevance of this study program to your intended career path after graduation?
How do you envision this specific course advancing your progress towards your ultimate career goals?

questions.question: How does your program prepare you for professional practice or registration in your home country or internationally?
questions.suggestedAnswer: This program is structured to meet international professional standards in {{field_of_study}}. Upon graduation, I will be eligible to pursue professional credentials and registrations such as {{field_of_study}} certifications, which are recognized in {{country_of_residence}} and internationally.
questions.guidance: Research the professional standards and certifications in your field for your home country.
questions.tooltip: Shows that your education has practical professional value beyond the degree itself.
variations: How will this program equip you for professional accreditation or practice in your home country or internationally?
What specific aspects of your chosen course of study prepare you for professional qualification or registration globally or in your native country?
To what extent does this curriculum prepare you for entry into professional practice or meeting licensing requirements in your country of origin or abroad?
Could you describe how your program will ready you for the professional standards and registration processes pertinent to your field, either domestically or internationally?

questions.question: Are you contributing any personal savings? If yes, what is the source?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{personal_savings_contribution}}
questions.guidance: Be honest about personal financial contributions. If you have savings, explain the legitimate source.
questions.tooltip: Personal contributions strengthen your financial profile and show commitment.
variations: Are you utilizing any of your own funds for this endeavor? If so, what is the origin of these savings?
Will you be contributing personal financial resources towards your studies? If yes, please specify their source.
Do you have personal savings that will be used to finance your education? If so, where do these funds come from?
Are you planning to fund a portion of your studies with your own assets? If applicable, could you disclose the source of these funds?

questions.question: Do you plan to work part-time while studying? How will you balance it with your studies?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{part_time_work_plans}} I understand that Canadian student visa regulations allow international students to work up to 20 hours per week during school terms, and I plan to strictly adhere to these limitations to prioritize my academic success.
questions.guidance: Explain your work plans realistically and demonstrate awareness of visa work restrictions.
questions.tooltip: Shows realistic planning and understanding of visa regulations and academic priorities.
variations: Do you intend to seek part-time employment during your studies? If so, what is your strategy for managing this alongside your academic commitments?
What are your plans regarding part-time work while you are studying? How will you ensure it doesn't interfere with your academic progress?
If you anticipate working part-time, how do you propose to balance these responsibilities with the demands of your coursework?
Are you considering part-time employment during your program? How will you maintain focus on your studies while working?

questions.question: Do you plan to use the post-study work visa (PSW)? If yes, how does it fit into your career plan?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{post_study_work_visa_plans ? 'I plan to {{post_study_work_visa_plans}}. However, after this temporary work period, I plan to return to {{country_of_residence}} and pursue my long-term career goals.' : 'I plan to return to {{country_of_residence}} immediately after completing my studies and begin my {{post_graduation_job_plans}}.'}} My ultimate goal is to {{post_graduation_job_plans}} in my home country.
questions.guidance: If planning to use PSW, explain how it's a stepping stone toward your ultimate career goals in your home country.
questions.tooltip: Post-study work is a temporary visa requiring clear connection to home country plans.
variations: Do you intend to apply for a post-study work visa? If so, how does this fit into your overall career development plan?
What are your plans concerning post-graduation work authorization? How will such an experience align with your professional goals?
If you choose to utilize the post-study work visa option, how will it serve your long-term career objectives?
Could you elaborate on how a potential post-study work period would integrate with your envisioned career trajectory?

questions.question: How will you manage healthcare while studying abroad?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{healthcare_management_abroad}} I understand that as an international student, I will have access to provincial health services in Canada and will register with a local healthcare provider upon arrival. I am aware of health insurance requirements for international students and will maintain appropriate coverage throughout my studies.
questions.guidance: Demonstrate knowledge of Canadian provincial health systems and planning for health emergencies.
questions.tooltip: Shows practical preparation and understanding of health support systems available.
variations: What arrangements have you made for your healthcare needs while studying abroad?
How do you plan to access medical services and manage your health coverage during your time as an international student?
Could you outline your strategy for ensuring you have adequate healthcare provision while pursuing your studies in this country?
What provisions have you put in place to manage any health-related matters while you are studying internationally?

questions.question: Do you understand the conditions of your student visa, including work limitations?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{student_visa_conditions_understanding}} I am aware that as a student visa holder in Canada, I am permitted to work up to 20 hours per week during school terms and full-time during official university breaks. I also understand the Post-Graduation Work Permit (PGWP) eligibility and conditions. I understand these are strict conditions and I am committed to complying with all visa regulations.
questions.guidance: Demonstrate clear understanding of specific UK student visa work restrictions and conditions.
questions.tooltip: Critical for credibility - shows you've researched visa obligations and plan to comply.
variations: Are you fully aware of the terms and conditions associated with your student visa, particularly regarding employment restrictions?
Do you understand the obligations and limitations of your student visa, including what you are permitted to do concerning work?
Are you familiar with the specific requirements of your student visa, especially those pertaining to part-time work or other employment?
Have you familiarized yourself with the stipulations of your student visa, including any guidelines on working while studying?

questions.question: What are your career goals after graduation?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{post_graduation_job_plans}} I plan to pursue this career after completing my {{intended_degree}}.
questions.guidance: Describe specific, realistic career goals aligned with your degree.
questions.tooltip: Clear post-graduation plans strengthen student visa application.
variations: What are your professional aspirations immediately following the completion of your degree?
Could you describe your intended career path once you graduate from this program?
What kind of professional roles or industries do you aim to pursue after obtaining your qualification?
What are your key career objectives that you hope to achieve upon receiving your degree?

questions.question: Do you plan to return to your home country after graduation?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{plan_to_return_home ? 'Yes, I plan to return to {{country_of_residence}} immediately after completing my studies to pursue my academic and career goals.' : 'I may initially pursue temporary work experience through a post-study work visa, but my long-term intention is to return to {{country_of_residence}}.'}} I have strong ties to my home country through my {{family_in_home_country ? 'close family members in {{country_of_residence}}' : 'professional and personal networks in {{country_of_residence}}'}} that make returning home a priority.
questions.guidance: Be honest about your intent. Strong home country ties provide credibility.
questions.tooltip: Intent to return is critical for student visa officer assessment.
variations: Do you intend to return to your country of origin after completing your academic program?
What are your plans for returning to your native country once you have graduated?
Will you be relocating back to your home country after the successful completion of your studies?
Do you envision returning to reside in your home country once your degree is conferred?

questions.question: What are your ties to your home country?
questions.suggestedAnswer: I have multiple strong ties to {{country_of_residence}} that make returning home important to me. {{family_in_home_country ? 'My family is based in {{country_of_residence}} - {{family_details}}.' : 'I have professional networks and personal commitments in {{country_of_residence}}.'}} {{property_or_assets_home ? 'Additionally, my family has property and assets in {{country_of_residence}}.' : ''}} After completing my {{intended_degree}}, I plan to {{post_graduation_job_plans}}.
questions.guidance: Mention family, property, business, and career opportunities.
questions.tooltip: Establishes personal and financial ties to home country.
variations: What strong connections or commitments do you have to your home country?
Could you articulate the personal, familial, or professional links you maintain with your native country?
What are the significant reasons or relationships that would encourage your return to your country of origin?
Please describe any substantial affiliations or responsibilities that bind you to your home country.

questions.question: Do you have close family in your home country?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{family_in_home_country ? 'Yes, I have close family in {{country_of_residence}}: {{family_details}}. These family ties are very important to me and provide strong motivation to return home after my studies.' : 'Most of my family is dispersed, but I have strong professional and personal connections to {{country_of_residence}} that are important to me.'}}
questions.guidance: Describe parents, siblings, or other family ties.
questions.tooltip: Critical indicator of home country attachment.
variations: Are your immediate family members residing in your country of origin?
Do you have immediate family dependents currently living in your native country?
Could you confirm if your immediate family still lives in your home country?
Where do your immediate family members currently reside?

questions.question: Do you have property or assets in your home country?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{property_or_assets_home ? 'Yes, my family owns property in {{country_of_residence}} that represents significant financial and personal wealth. This reinforces my commitment to eventually returning home.' : 'My family has investments and financial assets in {{country_of_residence}} that represent our financial ties to our home country.'}}
questions.guidance: Land, house, or business ownership shows financial ties.
questions.tooltip: Financial ties strengthen return home commitment.
variations: Are there any significant assets or properties you own in your country of origin?
Do you possess any real estate or substantial financial holdings in your native country?
Could you elaborate on any properties or significant assets you hold in your home country?
What financial ties, such as property or assets, do you maintain in your country of residence?

questions.question: Are you considering staying in Canada after graduation?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{consider_staying_ca ? 'I may pursue a post-study work visa to gain professional experience in Canada initially, as outlined in {{post_study_work_visa_plans}}, but my ultimate intention is to return to {{country_of_residence}}.' : 'My primary goal is to complete my studies and return to my home country to pursue my career goals.'}}
questions.guidance: If considering PSW or other work visas, frame as temporary work experience before returning home.
questions.tooltip: Short-term Canada work followed by home country return demonstrates genuine student intent.
variations: What are your plans following the completion of your studies in Canada?
Do you intend to remain in Canada once your academic program concludes?
Could you describe your intentions for after you graduate from your program here?
What are your long-term plans regarding your presence in Canada post-graduation?

questions.question: Have you visited Canada before?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{previous_ca_visits ? 'Yes, I have previously visited Canada, which gave me direct experience with the country and strengthened my commitment to studying here.' : 'No, I have not visited Canada before, but I have conducted thorough research about Canadian universities, the education system, and student life to prepare myself well for this transition.'}}
questions.guidance: Answer honestly about travel history.
questions.tooltip: Checks travel experience and visa history.
variations: Have you previously traveled to Canada?
Is this your first visit to Canada?
Do you have any prior history of travel to Canada?
Could you confirm if you have entered Canada on any previous occasions?

questions.question: When did you visit Canada and what was the purpose?
questions.suggestedAnswer: I visited {{primary_university}} and other Canadian cities to tour campuses, research academic programs, and experience university life firsthand. This visit greatly strengthened my commitment to studying at {{primary_university}} and helped me understand what to expect as an international student in Canada.
questions.guidance: Provide approximate dates and mention campus tours or tourism.
questions.tooltip: Verifies legitimate travel and interest in Canada.
variations: Please specify the dates and reasons for your previous trips to Canada?
Could you provide information on the timing and objective of your prior visits to Canada?
When did your last visit to Canada occur, and what was its primary purpose?
For your previous entry into Canada, what were the dates and the nature of your visit?

questions.question: Have you ever been denied any visa?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{visa_refusal_history ? 'Yes, I was previously denied a visa, and I understand this will be carefully reviewed by the visa officer.' : 'No, I have never been denied a visa to any country, and I have a clean immigration record.'}}
questions.guidance: Honesty is critical - this will be verified in your records.
questions.tooltip: Previous refusals are reviewed carefully.
variations: Have you ever experienced a visa rejection from any country?
Has an immigration authority ever refused you a visa?
Could you confirm if you have ever been denied entry or a visa to any nation?
Have there been any instances where a visa application of yours was not approved?

questions.question: Explain the visa refusal and what has changed.
questions.suggestedAnswer: I was previously denied a {{other_countries_visa_refusal ? 'visa to another country' : 'student visa'}} because {{visa_refusal_history ? 'of concerns regarding my documentation/financial capability/application details.' : ''}} Since then, my circumstances have significantly improved. I have: (1) Secured admission to {{primary_university}} with a strong academic profile, (2) Demonstrated comprehensive financial capability with bank statements and sponsor documentation, and (3) Established clear intent to study through detailed research and university engagement. I am now clearly eligible for a Canada student visa.
questions.guidance: Honestly explain the reason and how circumstances have improved.
questions.tooltip: Demonstrates addressing previous concerns.
variations: Could you elaborate on the circumstances of the visa refusal and what has since been addressed?
Please provide details regarding the denial and describe any relevant changes since then.
What led to that visa rejection, and how have those issues been resolved or changed?
Explain the reasons for the previous visa denial and outline what is different in your current situation.

questions.question: Do you have any criminal convictions?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{criminal_record ? 'I am required to disclose criminal history to Canadian Visas and Immigration (IRCC), and I have documentation regarding my circumstances.' : 'No, I do not have any criminal record, and I have maintained clean legal standing throughout my life.'}}
questions.guidance: Always answer truthfully - criminal history triggers security reviews.
questions.tooltip: Criminal history is grounds for visa denial.
variations: Have you ever been convicted of a criminal offense?
Do you have any past criminal convictions on record?

Have there been any instances where you were found guilty of a criminal act?

questions.question: Do you have any legal issues in Canada?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{any_ca_immigration_issues ? 'I have legal issues to disclose to the Canadian immigration authorities, and I have documentation prepared.' : 'No, I have no legal issues in Canada and intend to maintain full compliance with all Canadian laws as a student.'}}
questions.guidance: Disclose any arrests, legal violations, or previous Canadian visa issues if applicable.
questions.tooltip: Security clearance and admissibility requirement.

questions.question: Have you ever overstayed a visa in any country?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{previous_overstay ? 'Yes, I must disclose a previous visa overstay and understand this is a serious immigration matter that will be reviewed carefully by the Canadian authorities.' : 'No, I have never overstayed a visa and have maintained strict compliance with all visa terms and departure requirements.'}}
questions.guidance: Answer truthfully - overstays are serious immigration violations.
questions.tooltip: Overstays can result in visa denials and bans.

Have you ever remained in a country beyond the authorized period of your visa?

questions.question: Have you been denied a visa to any other country?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{other_countries_visa_refusal ? 'Yes, I have been denied visas to other countries, and I understand the Canadian authorities will review these decisions carefully.' : 'No, I have not been denied visas to other countries and maintain a strong visa and travel record.'}}
